title: TOPIK I Authentic Mock Exam 03 — Guided Course

courses.title: Everyday Replies and Greetings
objectives: Answer yes/no and wh- questions by echoing the right verb or question word
Use fixed reply phrases for apologies and the telephone

new_grammar.explanation: Past-tense polite question; answer by confirming or denying the same verb in the past.
new_grammar.example: 숙제를 다 했어요? - 네, 다 했어요.

new_grammar.explanation: Marks the time at which an action happens; required in answers to 언제/몇 시에 questions.
Attaches the reason to an apology or thanks; the pattern 늦어서 미안하다 is very common.

courses.review: You can now answer simple questions and reply correctly to apologies and phone calls.

courses.title: Where Am I? Places and Topics
objectives: Identify a place from the verbs and key words spoken there
Name the topic that covers a whole short conversation

new_grammar.explanation: Expresses the speaker's desire to do something.
Asks for or gives permission; the negative answer uses -(으)ㄹ 수 없다 or 안 되다.
new_grammar.example: 여기에서 전화해도 돼요? - 아니요, 할 수 없어요.

new_grammar.explanation: Invites the listener to decide something together.

courses.review: You can now pick out where a conversation happens and what it is about.

courses.title: Reading Basics: Particles, Verbs, Counters
objectives: Choose the right particle and counter to complete a sentence
Pick the verb, adverb, or connector that fits the context

new_grammar.explanation: Marks where an action verb takes place.
Connects a cause to its result; the second sentence happens because of the first.
Korean counts objects with a number plus a category-specific counter word.

courses.review: You can now fill blanks with the correct particle, counter, verb, and connector.

courses.title: Notices, Signs, and Asking the Way
objectives: Read a notice and spot the statement that does not match it
Follow and give simple directions, and handle a phone message

new_grammar.explanation: Within a period of time; marks a deadline window.
At the time of doing something.
Connects actions done in order, where the first leads into the second.

courses.review: You can now read notices for details and ask for or give directions politely.

courses.title: Appointments and Workplace Details
objectives: Track changes to a plan and keep the latest agreed details
Tell apart an action in progress, a deadline, and an intention

new_grammar.explanation: States what someone intends or is about to do.
Progressive: the action is happening right now.
After completing the first action, the second follows.

courses.review: You can now follow workplace dialogues and update plans as they change.

courses.title: Opinions, Advice, and Announcements
objectives: Find a speaker's main point after a contrast like 하지만
Catch the purpose and details of a public announcement

new_grammar.explanation: Two actions at the same time by the same subject.
Contrast connector; the speaker's real claim usually follows it.
new_grammar.example: 늦을 수 있어요. 하지만 전화를 해야 해요.

new_grammar.explanation: Brackets the start and end of a time span.

courses.review: You can now identify a speaker's opinion and the message of an announcement.

courses.title: Reading Paragraphs: Routines and Feelings
objectives: Separate the usual routine from the exception in a short essay
Read for a writer's habit, feeling, or main idea

new_grammar.explanation: States a decision or arrangement made earlier.
Even if/though: the action still happens despite the condition.
Contrasts the early stage with the present.

courses.review: You can now read short essays and tell routine from exception and feeling from fact.

courses.title: Sequencing and Campus Programs
objectives: Order scrambled sentences using connectors and counting clues
Read passages about clubs and programs for matching details

new_grammar.explanation: Comparison standard: later/earlier than N.
new_grammar.example: 약속 시간보다 삼십 분 늦게 도착했습니다.

new_grammar.explanation: 그런데 brings the unexpected problem; 그래서 gives the resulting action.
new_grammar.example: 그런데 일이 늦게 끝났습니다. 그래서 사과했습니다.

new_grammar.explanation: An action stops partway or switches to another.
new_grammar.example: 혼자 읽다가 끝까지 못 읽을 때가 많았습니다.

courses.review: You can now order a short story and read program notices for the right details.

courses.title: Letters and Longer Narratives
objectives: Read a polite letter for its purpose and key facts
Follow a multi-step narrative and choose the verb that fits the ending

new_grammar.explanation: For the benefit of: 외국인을 위한 교실 = a class for foreigners.
new_grammar.example: 외국인을 위한 무료 한국어 교실을 엽니다.

new_grammar.explanation: Marks a change: something has come to be the case.
Doing one thing reveals another.
new_grammar.example: 전화를 해 보니까 제 가방이 거기에 있었습니다.

courses.review: You can now read letters and longer stories and finish them with the right word.
